name: Managing Home
description: Helper instructions, deliveries, and building management

phrases.romanization: Gam¹ jat⁶ m⁴ goi¹ nei⁵ zyu² faan⁶
phrases.jyutping: gam1 jat6 m4 goi1 nei5 zyu2 faan6
phrases.context: Ask your helper to cook — a daily phrase in many HK households

phrases.romanization: Gam¹ jat⁶ m⁴ sai² zyu² faan⁶
phrases.jyutping: gam1 jat6 m4 sai2 zyu2 faan6
phrases.context: Let your helper know when you're eating out — saves grocery shopping
Instruct your helper to go to the wet market or supermarket

phrases.romanization: M⁴ goi¹ bong¹ ngo⁵ sai² saam¹
phrases.jyutping: m4 goi1 bong1 ngo5 sai2 saam1
phrases.context: Ask your helper to wash clothes
Ask your helper to mop the floors
Ask your helper to dust — HK air can be dusty

phrases.romanization: Gei³ dak¹ on³ si⁴ zip³ siu² pang⁴ jau⁵
phrases.jyutping: gei3 dak1 on3 si4 zip3 siu2 pang4 jau5
phrases.english: Remember to pick up the kids on time
phrases.context: Remind your helper about school pick-up

phrases.romanization: Jyu⁴ gwo² jau⁵ man⁶ tai⁴ da² ngo⁵ din⁶ waa²
phrases.jyutping: jyu4 gwo2 jau5 man6 tai4 da2 ngo5 din6 waa2
phrases.english: If there's a problem, call me
phrases.context: Leave instructions for your helper when you're out

phrases.romanization: Gam¹ jat⁶ jau⁵ faai³ dei⁶, m⁴ goi¹ bong¹ ngo⁵ sau¹
phrases.jyutping: gam1 jat6 jau5 faai3 dei6, m4 goi1 bong1 ngo5 sau1
phrases.chinese: 今日有快遞，唔該幫我收
phrases.english: There's a delivery today — please accept it for me
phrases.context: Let your helper know to accept a parcel when you're at work

phrases.romanization: M⁴ hou² bei² jan⁴ jap⁶ lei⁴
phrases.jyutping: m4 hou2 bei2 jan4 jap6 lei4
phrases.context: A safety instruction for your helper when you're not home

phrases.romanization: Cing² man⁶ hai⁶ m⁴ hai⁶ mat⁶ jip⁶ gun² lei⁵?
phrases.jyutping: cing2 man6 hai6 m4 hai6 mat6 jip6 gun2 lei5
phrases.context: How to start a call to your building's management office

phrases.romanization: Ngo⁵ hai⁶ X joh⁶ Y sat¹
phrases.jyutping: ngo5 hai6 X zo6 Y sat1
phrases.english: I'm in Block X, Flat Y
phrases.context: Identify your flat when reporting a problem to management

phrases.english: My flat has a water leak
phrases.context: Report a water leak to building management — unfortunately common in HK
A critical report in HK's humid heat — report this immediately
Report a blocked toilet to management or call a plumber

phrases.romanization: Gei² si⁴ ho² ji³ lei⁴ tai²?
phrases.jyutping: gei2 si4 ho2 ji3 lei4 tai2
phrases.english: When can someone come to look at it?
phrases.context: Ask when a repair person can come — follow up if they say 'later'

phrases.romanization: Cing² man⁶ jiu³ m⁴ jiu³ cim¹ meng²?
phrases.jyutping: cing2 man6 jiu3 m4 jiu3 cim1 meng2
phrases.context: Ask the delivery person if a signature is required

phrases.romanization: Fong³ hai² mun⁴ hau² zau⁶ hou²
phrases.jyutping: fong3 hai2 mun4 hau2 zau6 hou2
phrases.english: Leaving it at the door is fine
phrases.context: Tell a delivery person to leave the parcel outside your door

phrases.romanization: Cing² man⁶ jau⁵ mou⁵ wai⁴ sau¹ fuk⁶ mou⁶?
phrases.jyutping: cing2 man6 jau5 mou5 wai4 sau1 fuk6 mou6
phrases.english: Do you have a repair service?
phrases.context: Ask building management if they can send someone to fix something
Report a burned-out lightbulb
Report a broken lock — a security priority
Report a broken elevator to building management
Specify what dish you'd like for dinner — follow with the dish name
Tell your helper what you'd like to eat — swap for your preference
Specify cooking preferences for your helper

phrases.romanization: Gam¹ jat⁶ mou⁵ je⁵ zou⁶, fong³ gaa³ laa³
phrases.jyutping: gam1 jat6 mou5 je5 zou6, fong3 gaa3 laa3
phrases.english: Nothing to do today — you have the day off
phrases.context: Give your helper a day off — a very appreciated thing to say

phrases.romanization: Nei⁵ gam¹ jat⁶ zou⁶ dak¹ hou² hou²
phrases.jyutping: nei5 gam1 jat6 zou6 dak1 hou2 hou2
phrases.english: You did a great job today
phrases.context: Praise your helper — positive feedback goes a long way
A warm thank you to your helper at the end of the day

dialogueScenes.title: Giving instructions to your helper
dialogueScenes.description: You're heading to work and telling your helper the plan for the day

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 今日唔該你煮飯，我想食燒雞。
turns.jyutping: gam1 jat6 m4 goi1 nei5 zyu2 faan6, ngo5 soeng2 sik6 siu1 gai1.
turns.romanization: Gam¹ jat⁶ m⁴ goi¹ nei⁵ zyu² faan⁶, ngo⁵ soeng² sik⁶ siu¹ gai¹.
turns.english: Could you cook dinner today — I'd like roast chicken.

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: hou2, dak1. zung6 jau5 me1?
turns.romanization: Hou², dak¹. Zung⁶ jau⁵ me¹?
turns.english: Okay, sure. Anything else?

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 今日去買餸先。記得接小朋友放學。
turns.jyutping: gam1 jat6 heoi3 maai5 sung3 sin1. gei3 dak1 zip3 siu2 pang4 jau5 fong3 hok6.
turns.romanization: Gam¹ jat⁶ heoi³ maai⁵ sung³ sin¹. Gei³ dak¹ zip³ siu² pang⁴ jau⁵ fong³ hok⁶.
turns.english: Go buy groceries first. Remember to pick up the kids from school.

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: hou2, ngo5 zi1 laa3. fong3 hok6 gei2 dim2?
turns.romanization: Hou², ngo⁵ zi¹ laa³. Fong³ hok⁶ gei² dim²?
turns.english: Okay, I understand. What time does school finish?

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 三點半。如果有問題打我電話。
turns.jyutping: saam1 dim2 bun3. jyu4 gwo2 jau5 man6 tai4 da2 ngo5 din6 waa2.
turns.romanization: Saam¹ dim² bun³. Jyu⁴ gwo² jau⁵ man⁶ tai⁴ da² ngo⁵ din⁶ waa².
turns.english: Three thirty. If there's a problem, call me.

dialogueScenes.title: Calling building management about a repair
dialogueScenes.description: Your AC has broken down and you need to report it

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: cing2 man6 hai6 m4 hai6 mat6 jip6 gun2 lei5?
turns.romanization: Cing² man⁶ hai⁶ m⁴ hai⁶ mat⁶ jip⁶ gun² lei⁵?
turns.english: Is this building management?

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: hai6, cing2 man6 hai6 bin1 wai2?
turns.romanization: Hai⁶, cing² man⁶ hai⁶ bin¹ wai²?
turns.english: Yes, may I ask who's calling?

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 我係A座十八樓B室。我屋企冷氣壞咗。
turns.jyutping: ngo5 hai6 A zo6 sap6 baat3 lau4 B sat1. ngo5 uk1 kei2 laang5 hei3 waai6 zo2.
turns.romanization: Ngo⁵ hai⁶ A zo⁶ sap⁶ baat³ lau⁴ B sat¹. Ngo⁵ uk¹ kei² laang⁵ hei³ waai⁶ zo².
turns.english: I'm in Block A, Floor 18, Flat B. My AC is broken.

turns.speaker: other
turns.chinese: 好，我哋安排維修員，下午嚟睇。
turns.jyutping: hou2, ngo5 dei6 on1 paai4 wai4 sau1 jyun4, haa6 zau3 lei4 tai2.
turns.romanization: Hou², ngo⁵ dei⁶ on¹ paai⁴ wai⁴ sau¹ jyun⁴, haa⁶ zau³ lei⁴ tai².
turns.english: Okay, we'll arrange a repair person to come this afternoon.

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: gei2 si4 ho2 ji3 lei4 tai2?
turns.romanization: Gei² si⁴ ho² ji³ lei⁴ tai²?
turns.english: What time can they come?

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: haa6 zau3 loeng5 dim2 zo2 jau6.
turns.romanization: Haa⁶ zau³ loeng⁵ dim² zo² jau⁶.
turns.english: Around 2pm.

name: Talking to Kids
description: Morning routines, praise, meals, and bedtime

phrases.english: Wake up! / Time to get up!
phrases.context: The morning battle cry in every HK household
Tell the kids to eat their breakfast before school
The most-used parenting phrase — works on all ages
The morning routine command
Morning and bedtime essential
Tell the kids to put their clothes on
Get the kids ready to leave — the final step before the door

phrases.english: Finish your food / Eat it all up
phrases.context: Tell children to finish their plate — said at every meal
The universal mealtime correction
The classic HK parent phrase — said at meals, in the car, everywhere
Tidy-up time — a daily after-play routine
沖涼 is the uniquely Cantonese word for bathing — don't say 洗澡 which is more Mandarin

phrases.english: Brush teeth and go to sleep
phrases.context: The bedtime double command — said together as one phrase
Praise a child — 叻仔 for boys, 叻女 for girls, or just 好叻 for either
Encourage children for any achievement or good behaviour
Comfort a crying child
Reassure a child after a fall or fright
Teach children to apologise — a very important phrase for kids to learn
Teach children to say please — use when asking for something
Tell your child you love them — increasingly said in HK families

dialogueScenes.title: The morning rush
dialogueScenes.description: Getting the kids ready for school on a typical morning

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: hei2 san1 laa3! faai3 di1!
turns.romanization: Hei² san¹ laa³! Faai³ di¹!
turns.english: Wake up! Hurry!

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: zung6 mei6 hai6 si4 gaan3 aa3.
turns.romanization: Zung⁶ mei⁶ hai⁶ si⁴ gaan³ aa³.
turns.english: It's not time yet.

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: zeon2 bei6 faan1 hok6 laa1! caat3 ngaa4, zoek3 saam1!
turns.romanization: Zeon² bei⁶ faan¹ hok⁶ laa¹! Caat³ ngaa⁴, zoek³ saam¹!
turns.english: Get ready for school! Brush teeth, get dressed!

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: hou2 laa3 hou2 laa3.
turns.romanization: Hou² laa³ hou² laa³.
turns.english: Okay okay.

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: sik6 zou2 caan1 sin1! sik6 saai3 sin1 zau2.
turns.romanization: Sik⁶ zou² caan¹ sin¹! Sik⁶ saai³ sin¹ zau².
turns.english: Eat breakfast first! Finish it before we go.

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 瞓覺時間喇！收拾玩具先。
turns.jyutping: fan3 gaau3 si4 gaan3 laa3! sau1 sap1 waan4 geoi6 sin1.
turns.romanization: Fan³ gaau³ si⁴ gaan³ laa³! Sau¹ sap¹ waan⁴ geoi⁶ sin¹.
turns.english: Bedtime! Put your toys away first.

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: ngo5 m4 soeng2 fan3.
turns.romanization: Ngo⁵ m⁴ soeng² fan³.
turns.english: I don't want to sleep.

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: cung1 loeng4, jin4 hau6 caat3 ngaa4 fan3 gaau3.
turns.romanization: Cung¹ loeng⁴, jin⁴ hau⁶ caat³ ngaa⁴ fan³ gaau³.
turns.english: Shower, then brush teeth and sleep.

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: hou2 laa3. maan5 on1.
turns.romanization: Hou² laa³. Maan⁵ on¹.
turns.english: Okay. Good night.

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: maan5 on1. ngo5 oi3 nei5.
turns.romanization: Maan⁵ on¹. Ngo⁵ oi³ nei⁵.
turns.english: Good night. I love you.

phrases.context: Tell the doctor or pharmacist you're unwell — a good starting phrase
Report a headache — 頭 (head) + 痛 (pain) for any head pain
Report stomach pain — 肚 is the Cantonese word for stomach
Report a fever — the doctor will take your temperature
Report a cough — very common in HK
Report nasal symptoms
Report nausea — also useful for seasickness or motion sickness
Point to where it hurts — essential when you can't name the body part
Communicate severity of pain

phrases.english: How long has it been hurting?
phrases.context: The doctor will ask you this — know the answer
Report a fall or injury

words.english: fell / hurt myself in a fall

phrases.english: Can you give me some medicine?
phrases.context: Ask the doctor or pharmacist for medication
Ask about the dosage
Ask when to take the medication
The most common dosage instruction you'll hear
Instruction to take medicine after eating

phrases.romanization: Jyu⁴ gwo² m⁴ hou² zyun³ faan¹ lei⁴
phrases.jyutping: jyu4 gwo2 m4 hou2 zyun3 faan1 lei4
phrases.english: If it doesn't get better, come back
phrases.context: What the doctor says at the end of every appointment — be ready to hear this

phrases.romanization: Ngo⁵ jau⁵ gwok³ joek⁶ gwo³ man⁵
phrases.jyutping: ngo5 jau5 gwok3 joek6 gwo3 man5
phrases.context: Critical information to tell any doctor or pharmacist

dialogueScenes.title: Describing symptoms to a doctor
dialogueScenes.description: You visit a clinic and explain what's wrong

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: nei5 m4 syu1 fuk6 aa3? jau5 me1 man6 tai4?
turns.romanization: Nei⁵ m⁴ syu¹ fuk⁶ aa³? Jau⁵ me¹ man⁶ tai⁴?
turns.english: You're not feeling well? What's the problem?

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 我唔舒服。我頭痛，又發燒。
turns.jyutping: ngo5 m4 syu1 fuk6. ngo5 tau4 tung3, jau6 faat3 siu1.
turns.romanization: Ngo⁵ m⁴ syu¹ fuk⁶. Ngo⁵ tau⁴ tung³, jau⁶ faat³ siu¹.
turns.english: I'm not feeling well. I have a headache and a fever.

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: tung3 zo2 gei2 noi6?
turns.romanization: Tung³ zo² gei² noi⁶?
turns.english: How long has it been hurting?

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: tung3 zo2 loeng5 jat6.
turns.romanization: Tung³ zo² loeng⁵ jat⁶.
turns.english: For two days.

turns.speaker: other
turns.chinese: 我俾藥你，一日食三次，食完飯先食。如果唔好，轉番嚟。
turns.jyutping: ngo5 bei2 joek6 nei5, jat1 jat6 sik6 saam1 ci3, sik6 zo2 faan6 sin1 sik6. jyu4 gwo2 m4 hou2, zyun3 faan1 lei4.
turns.romanization: Ngo⁵ bei² joek⁶ nei⁵, jat¹ jat⁶ sik⁶ saam¹ ci³, sik⁶ zo² faan⁶ sin¹ sik⁶. Jyu⁴ gwo² m⁴ hou², zyun³ faan¹ lei⁴.
turns.english: I'll give you medicine — three times a day, after meals. If you don't improve, come back.

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 明白。一日三次。多謝醫生。
turns.jyutping: ming4 baak6. jat1 jat6 saam1 ci3. do1 ze6 ji1 sang1.
turns.romanization: Ming⁴ baak⁶. Jat¹ jat⁶ saam¹ ci³. Do¹ ze⁶ ji¹ sang¹.
turns.english: I understand. Three times a day. Thank you doctor.

dialogueScenes.title: At the pharmacy
dialogueScenes.description: You need medicine from the chemist

turns.speaker: user
turns.chinese: 唔該，我唔舒服。我咳嗽，流鼻水。
turns.jyutping: m4 goi1, ngo5 m4 syu1 fuk6. ngo5 kat1 sau3, lau4 pei5 seoi2.
turns.romanization: M⁴ goi¹, ngo⁵ m⁴ syu¹ fuk⁶. Ngo⁵ kat¹ sau³, lau⁴ pei⁵ seoi².
turns.english: Excuse me, I'm not feeling well. I have a cough and a runny nose.

turns.speaker: other
turns.jyutping: nei5 jau5 mou5 joek6 mat6 gwo3 man5?
turns.romanization: Nei⁵ jau⁵ mou⁵ joek⁶ mat⁶ gwo³ man⁵?
turns.english: Do you have any drug allergies?

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: mou5 joek6 mat6 gwo3 man5.
turns.romanization: Mou⁵ joek⁶ mat⁶ gwo³ man⁵.
turns.english: No drug allergies.

turns.speaker: other
turns.chinese: 好，呢個咳藥，一日食三次，食完飯先食。
turns.jyutping: hou2, ni1 go3 kat1 joek6, jat1 jat6 sik6 saam1 ci3, sik6 zo2 faan6 sin1 sik6.
turns.romanization: Hou², ni¹ go³ kat¹ joek⁶, jat¹ jat⁶ sik⁶ saam¹ ci³, sik⁶ zo² faan⁶ sin¹ sik⁶.
turns.english: Okay, here's cough medicine — three times a day, after meals.

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: sik6 gei2 do1 nap1?
turns.romanization: Sik⁶ gei² do¹ nap¹?
turns.english: How many tablets each time?

turns.speaker: other
turns.chinese: 每次食兩粒。多喝水，好快好㗎。
turns.jyutping: mui5 ci3 sik6 loeng5 nap1. do1 hot3 seoi2, hou2 faai3 hou2 gaa3.
turns.romanization: Mui⁵ ci³ sik⁶ loeng⁵ nap¹. Do¹ hot³ seoi², hou² faai³ hou² gaa³.
turns.english: Two tablets each time. Drink plenty of water — you'll feel better soon.

name: Groceries
description: Supermarket basics, finding items, and checkout

phrases.context: Ask a staff member where to find an item — follow with the item name
The answer you'll get when asking where something is

phrases.romanization: Ni¹ go³ gei² si⁴ dou³ kei⁴?
phrases.jyutping: ni1 go3 gei2 si4 dou3 kei4
phrases.context: Check the expiry date — important for fresh products

phrases.romanization: Jau⁵ mou⁵ jau¹ gei¹ sou³ coi³?
phrases.jyutping: jau5 mou5 jau1 gei1 sou3 coi3
phrases.context: Ask for organic produce — increasingly available in HK supermarkets
Ask for fresher produce when what's on display looks tired

phrases.romanization: Ni¹ go³ jau⁵ mou⁵ zit³ kau³?
phrases.jyutping: ni1 go3 jau5 mou5 zit3 kau3
phrases.context: Ask if an item has a discount
Present your coupon at the checkout
Ask the total at checkout

phrases.romanization: Ho² m⁴ ho² ji³ jung⁶ baat³ daat⁶ tung¹?
phrases.jyutping: ho2 m4 ho2 ji3 jung6 baat3 daat6 tung1
phrases.context: Octopus is widely accepted at supermarkets in HK
Ask about loyalty points — most HK supermarkets have schemes
Decline the plastic bag — HK charges for plastic bags
Ask to return an item — have your receipt ready

phrases.romanization: M⁴ goi¹, mai⁴ gaa³ loeng⁵ go³
phrases.jyutping: m4 goi1 mai4 gaa3 loeng5 go3

phrases.romanization: Cing² man⁶, ji¹ san¹ jyun⁴ hai⁶ bin¹ dou⁶?

phrases.english: Excuse me, where is the baby section?
phrases.context: Find baby products in a large supermarket

dialogueScenes.title: Finding an item in the supermarket
dialogueScenes.description: You can't find what you're looking for

turns.speaker: user
turns.jyutping: m4 goi1, gam3 lam4 jau4 hai6 bin1 dou6?
turns.romanization: M⁴ goi¹, gam³ lam⁴ jau⁴ hai⁶ bin¹ dou⁶?

turns.speaker: other

turns.speaker: user

turns.speaker: user
